rationale: Flow logs enable the investigation of incidents involving unauthorized network traffic, such as an attacker exfiltrating data or pivoting to other hosts. A VPC Flow Log can be created at the VPC, subnet, or network-interface level, and a VPC-level flow log captures traffic for every subnet in that VPC. This finding flags a subnet only when neither the subnet itself nor its parent VPC has a flow log, so a subnet already covered by a VPC-level flow log is not reported.
remediation: Create a flow log covering the subnet - either directly on the subnet, or once at the VPC level to cover every subnet in the VPC.
